comments: Injured individual on the old scrapes
With a Eurasian on the new lagoons. Vertical white line visible. Photographed.
2 with a pair of curlew flying NE coming from the new lagoons.
3 young in nest looking very ready to fledge
Late leaving individual. Grey backed. Photographed.

We were walking along the road when we heard a repeated shreaking noise we entered the wooded are and followed the noise. It stopped when we got near the all of a sudden a short eared owl bolted out of a tree. We noticed it's yellow eyes as it flew towards, then over us and disappeared into more trees.
Several females with recently fledged young
Not including breeding birds in nearby Dunbar harbour

comments: Female in thick heather/moss/grass about 150m from track. Behaving as if it had chicks in tow, but vegetation meant I could only see head and neck. Heathaze didnt help either!
Male on fenceline by entrance track
Far end. Seen via scope. Too far to photograph.

Subspecies undetermined - 2cy with strikingly clean white head, neck and underparts, and slate-grey markings to feathers on upperparts. Quite different to most graellsii at this age, so intermedius might be a possibility? Photographed
2 on passage far away looking North
Seen along City of Edinburgh Bypass just north of site. Possibly a hybrid
2 adults, 1 of which colour ringed but too distant to read code
Pair nesting in lighthouse garden and another male near wire dump
